basic_details.job_title: Bombay High Court Cook-cum-Attendant Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.short_information: The Bombay High Court has officially published the Cook-cum-Attendant Recruitment 2026 notification on its official website bombayhighcourt.gov.in. The recruitment drive aims to fill 5 Cook-cum-Attendant positions. Candidates within the age range of 18 to 38 years and holding a minimum 7th Standard pass along with knowledge of Marathi language (reading, writing, and speaking) and relevant cooking experience are eligible to apply. The application process will be conducted in offline mode and will remain active until 06 July 2026. Eligible candidates can download the official notification and ac

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 38 years Age Relaxation: Up to 43 years for reserved categories; no upper age limit for serving Court/Government employees.

qualification.education_qualification: Minimum 7th Standard Pass, Knowledge of Marathi language (read, write and speak), Cooking experience
qualification.details: Minimum 7th Standard Pass. Knowledge of Marathi language (read, write and speak). Cooking experience. Preference for a cooking course certificate from a recognized institute.

salary_details.details: Pay Matrix Level S-3: Rs. 16600-52400/- Per Month plus Dearness Allowance and other allowances as per rules.

selection_process: Written Examination Practical Cooking Test Physical Efficiency Test Interview
how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: bombayhighcourt.gov.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 06-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

all_official_links.label: Download Bombay High Court Cook-cum-Attendant Job 2026 Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Bombay High Court Cook-cum-Attendant recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to receive the application for the Bombay High Court Cook-cum-Attendant recruitment is 06 July 2026. Ensure your application reaches the specified address by this date.

faq.question: Q2. What are the minimum educational qualifications required for the Cook-cum-Attendant post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed at least the 7th Standard. Additionally, knowledge of the Marathi language (reading, writing, and speaking) and relevant cooking experience are mandatory.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for applying to this position?
faq.answer: The age limit for the Cook-cum-Attendant post is between 18 years and 38 years. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per government rules.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are available for the Cook-cum-Attendant position?
faq.answer: There are a total of 5 vacancies for the Cook-cum-Attendant post announced by the Bombay High Court in this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Cook-cum-Attendant role?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a salary in the Pay Matrix Level S-3, ranging from Rs. 16600 to Rs. 52400 per month, along with Dearness Allowance and other applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application mode for the Bombay High Court Cook-cum-Attendant recruitment is offline. Candidates need to download the form and send it via post.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Cook-cum-Attendant post?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Written Examination, a Practical Cooking Test, a Physical Efficiency Test, and an Interview.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any preference given for specific qualifications?
faq.answer: Yes, preference will be given to candidates who possess a cooking course certificate from a recognized institute.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for the Cook-cum-Attendant post?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 1000/- for General/OBC and Rs. 1000/- for SC/ST categories.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official notification and application form can be downloaded from the official website of the Bombay High Court. Links are provided in the "Important Links" section.
